type: SURVEY
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
note: Adding new 'hash_sha256 = Regex(...)' constant near the end of outlines/types/__init__.py (after isbn). Purely additive - just appending a new top-level assignment. Let me know what construct(s) you're touching in this file so we can avoid clobbering.

type: DRAFT
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
region: end of file, appended after isbn definition
code: ssn = Regex(r"[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{4}")

hash_sha256 = Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")
note: Merged tail: ssn added first (immediately after isbn), then hash_sha256 right after it. Both are standalone top-level Regex assignments, no shared lines otherwise.

type: DRAFT
files: outlines/types/__init__.py
region: end-of-file additions after isbn
code: isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

# Matches a US Social Security Number (SSN) in the format XXX-XX-XXXX
ssn = Regex(r"\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}")

# Matches a SHA-256 hash: 64 hexadecimal characters
hash_sha256 = Regex(r"[a-fA-F0-9]{64}")
note: Proposing order: isbn (unchanged) -> ssn -> hash_sha256, each with a one-line comment. Adjust ssn comment/regex to your liking and reply with your own DRAFT if different; then we both AGREE on identical final text.
